Standard Deduction Amount, Tax Years 1970-2024
Year Single Head of Household Married
1970 $1,100 $1,100 $1,100 1997 $4,150 $6,050 $6,900
1971 $1,050 $1,050 $1,050 1998 $4,250 $6,250 $7,100
1972 $1,300 $1,300 $1,300 1999 $4,300 $6,350 $7,200
1973 $1,300 $1,300 $1,300 2000 $4,400 $6,450 $7,350
1974 $1,300 $1,300 $1,300 2001 $4,550 $6,650 $7,600
1975 $1,600 $1,600 $1,900 2002 $4,700 $6,900 $7,850
1976 $1,700 $1,700 $2,100 2003 $4,750 $7,000 $9,500
1977 $2,200 $2,200 $3,200 2004 $4,850 $7,150 $9,700
1978 $2,200 $2,200 $3,200 2005 $5,000 $7,300 $10,000
1979 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2006 $5,150 $7,550 $10,300
1980 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2007 $5,350 $7,850 $10,700
1981 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2008 $5,450 $8,000 $10,900
1982 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2009 $5,700 $8,350 $11,400
1983 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2010 $5,700 $8,400 $11,400
1984 $2,300 $2,300 $3,400 2011 $5,800 $8,500 $11,600
1985 $2,400 $2,400 $3,550 2012 $5,950 $8,700 $11,900
1986 $2,480 $2,480 $3,670 2013 $6,100 $8,950 $12,200
1987 $2,540 $2,540 $3,760 2014 $6,200 $9,100 $12,400
1988 $3,000 $4,400 $5,000 2015 $6,300 $9,250 $12,600
1989 $3,100 $4,550 $5,200 2016 $6,300 $9,300 $12,600
1990 $3,250 $4,750 $5,450 2017 $6,350 $9,350 $12,700
1991 $3,400 $5,000 $5,700 2018 $12,000 $18,000 $24,000
1992 $3,600 $5,250 $6,000 2019 $12,200 $18,350 $24,400
1993 $3,700 $5,450 $6,200 2020 $12,400 $18,650 $24,800
1994 $3,800 $5,600 $6,350 2021 $12,550 $18,800 $25,100
1995 $3,900 $5,750 $6,550 2022 $12,950 $19,400 $25,900
1996 $4,000 $5,900 $6,700 2023 $13,850 $20,800 $27,700
2024 $14,600 $21,900 $29,200 2025 $14,600 $21,900 $29,200
Notes:
Standard deduction for married separately is the same as single
Sources:
IRS Revenue Procedures, various years.
Standard Deduction Amount, Tax Years 1970-2024
Historical Statistics of the United States, Colonial Period-1970
 The standard deduction was first adopted in 1944. From 1944-1969, it was equal to 10 percent of adjusted
gross income, up to a maximum of $1,000.
IRS Form 1040 instructions
Bakija and Steuerle, National Tax Journal, 1991
Beginning in 1985, the blind and those aged 65 or over received larger standard deductions.
Pechman, J.A., Federal Tax Policy
